pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take:
**If they put people like "The Backpass Assassin" in charge, it makes me wonder if anyone even knows what soccer is anymore.**

When you’ve got a manager like Gunner, aka "Bubba G," who understands soccer enough to draft France, the winning results are inevitable. France’s 3-0 triumph over Sweden says it all. Compare that to Gayden’s pitiful setup—England against DR Congo? You can see how the term "Backpass Assassin" perfectly describes a guy who drafts like a turtle in hibernation. It’s like he just picked countries from FIFA 23 without considering actual tactics. England will stumble, and "Bubba G" will just keep on cruising.

Devin, or "Ghost Pepper," has the strategic brain that only drafting both USA and Bosnia can reveal—it's either a stroke of genius or complete oversight. Fortunately for him, the USA is cracking form, and he’ll collect points like I used to collect yellow cards. Let’s get real: Bosnia is just a freebie Devin gave himself. Expect "Ghost Pepper" to sit pretty high and mighty by the end of the day, the mirror opposite of Gayden’s hopeless escapades. When you're left with Zach and Rafe, both scrambling in the middle of the pack, you realize there’s only one true maestro in this charade: Gunner is your man.

pundit_takes.headline: The Backpass Assassin's England Set to Misfire Against Bubba G's DR Congo
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden expected to rely on son's strategy of hoping for miracles.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper Positions USA Against Bosnia In Tactical High-Five Session
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin hopes new formation where everyone plays forward will confuse opponents.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy Zach Relies on Saudi Arabia's Magic to Climb Rankings
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach wonders if mustard can be imported for spirit boost.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G's Senegal Poised to Surpass Belgian Waffles in Santa Clara
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner claims points are tastier when churro fries follow the match.
